page: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs, consisting of 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a base clock speed of 2.4 GHz and supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. The platform includes 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is a key highlight, featuring 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is delivered via a 25 Gbps baseline interface managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and data decompression. Uncached large-block memory read and write operations rank in the top 25%, supported by the DDR5 architecture. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance, alongside top-tier single-core Geekbench scores and image processing capabilities. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions enable strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large LLM models, though text generation on large 70B models remains a bottleneck, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7i.8xlarge offers a high-density combination of fast DDR5 memory and massive local NVMe SSD storage, reducing the need for external network-attached storage overhead. This hardware profile is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, large-scale data decompression pipelines, static web serving, and localized machine learning inference tasks that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core execution and image processing, alongside strong uncached memory bandwidth and data decompression throughput. The CPU supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX extensions, delivering strong prompt processing for large language models, though text generation for massive models is limited. This server is highly efficient for workloads requiring high-density local storage and fast memory access, making it a strong fit for transactional databases, data indexing, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.8xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 7500 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining high-capacity local NVMe SSDs with DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix extensions for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM
Bundled with 7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads
Delivers strong uncached memory bandwidth and high-throughput data decompression
Best suited for transactional databases, static web serving, and localized LLM prompt processing
